function.name: create_plugin
function.description: Scaffold a new Evonic plugin with the proper directory structure, manifest, handler, routes, and templates. The plugin is immediately hot-loaded after creation.

id.description: Unique plugin identifier (alphanumeric, dashes, underscores only). Must match the directory name. Example: 'my_plugin' or 'weather-widget'.

description.description: What the plugin does. Example: 'Shows current weather on the dashboard'.

plugin_type.description: Plugin type: 'event_only' for event handlers only (no HTTP routes), 'routes_only' for HTTP endpoints only (no event handlers), 'full' for both.

events.description: Events to subscribe to. Required for event_only and full types. Each event maps to an on_<event> handler function in handler.py.

nav_label.description: If provided, adds a navbar link to the plugin's main page. Example: 'Weather'. Only applicable when plugin_type is 'routes_only' or 'full'.

nav_path.description: URL path for the navbar link. Defaults to '/<plugin_id>' if not specified. Example: '/weather/dashboard'.

variables.description: Configurable plugin variables shown in the plugin settings UI.
